---
title: Matti Vuorre
title-block-banner: false
comments: false
---

I am a research psychologist interested in psychological functioning especially in the context of digital technologies. In my work, I apply statistical methods to large-scale datasets and conduct controlled experiments in order to understand cognition, behavior, and well-being both on- and offline. For more information, you can take a look at my [vita](https://github.com/mvuorre/CV/raw/main/vuorre-matti.pdf), check out my Tilburg profile [here](https://www.tilburguniversity.edu/staff/m-j-vuorre/), or browse this website.

I serve as a recommender for [PCI: Psych](https://psych.peercommunityin.org/) and [PCI: Registered Reports](https://rr.peercommunityin.org/), am a member of the [PsyArXiv](https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/) steering committee, and am on the editorial board of the Center for Open Science's [Instagram Data Access Pilot for Well-being Research](https://www.cos.io/meta).

::: grid
::: {.g-col-6}
## Employment
- Tilburg University (2023 - now)\
  *Assistant Professor*
- University of Oxford (2020 - 2022)\
  *Postdoctoral researcher*
- Columbia University (2018 - 2020)\
  *Postdoctoral Scientist*
:::
::: {.g-col-6}
## Education
- PhD (2018)\
  *Columbia University*
- MPhil (2017)\
  *Columbia University*
- MA (2015)\
  *Columbia University*
- BSc (Hons) (2013)\
  *Victoria University of Wellington*
:::
:::

## My links

[My website](https://vuorre.com) | [GitHub](https://github.com/mvuorre) | [BlueSky](https://bsky.app/profile/matti.vuorre.com) | [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-5052-066X) | [PCI: Psych](https://psych.peercommunityin.org/public/user_public_page?userId=196) | [PCI: RR](https://rr.peercommunityin.org/public/user_public_page?userId=1570) | [PREreview](https://prereview.org/profiles/0000-0001-5052-066X) | [OSF](https://osf.io/tdyix/) | [Mastodon](https://bayes.club/@matti) | [CV](https://github.com/mvuorre/CV/raw/main/vuorre-matti.pdf) | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/matti-vuorre) | [Tilburg University website](https://www.tilburguniversity.edu/staff/m-j-vuorre) | [email](mailto:m.j.vuorre@tilburguniversity.edu)

## Blogroll

[Statistical Modeling, Causal Inference, and Social Science](https://statmodeling.stat.columbia.edu/) | [The 100% CI](https://www.the100.ci/) | [Get Syeducated](https://getsyeducated.substack.com/) | [Alex Holcombe's blog](https://alexholcombe.wordpress.com/) | [Dorothy Bishop](https://deevybee.blogspot.com/) | [Andrew Heiss](https://www.andrewheiss.com/blog/) | [Elements of Evolutionary Anthropology](https://elevanth.org/) | [A. Solomon Kurz](https://solomonkurz.netlify.app/blog/) | [Ruben C. Arslan](https://rubenarslan.github.io/) | [Computational Psychology](http://haines-lab.com/#posts) | [Tobias Dienlin](https://tobiasdienlin.com/blog/) | [Making it as an early career academic](https://gavinbuckingham.wordpress.com/) | [Jan Vanhove](https://janhove.github.io/) | [Nick Ballou](https://nickballou.com/blog/) | [Steve Haroz](http://steveharoz.com/blog/) | [Lorne Campbell](https://www.lornecampbell.org/) | [Tidyverse](https://www.tidyverse.org/blog/) | [Robert Kubinec](https://www.robertkubinec.com/) | [Never Met a Science](https://kevinmunger.substack.com/) | [*[Suggest a link](https://github.com/mvuorre/mvuorre.github.io/issues)*]

## Integrity statement

Academic research is a service conducted in the public interest without biases or hidden agendas. I aim to do so and conduct my work in accordance with [The European Code of Conduct for Research Integrity](https://allea.org/code-of-conduct/) and its four main principles: Reliability, Honesty, Respect, and Accountability.

I list all the sources of my research funding in [my CV](https://github.com/mvuorre/CV/raw/main/vuorre-matti.pdf), and all my current external positions on my [employer's website](https://www.tilburguniversity.edu/staff/m-j-vuorre). My work is or has been funded by the European Research Council, the Economic and Social Research Council, the Huo Family Foundation, Tilburg University, and the University of Oxford's John Fell Fund. I have received honoraria (or similar) for services relating to research and education from the European Commission, University of Zürich, University of Aberdeen, and the University of Basel. I have provided in-kind consultations and served as a non-paid panel member for Meta and K-Games.

## About me

Besides thinking about minds, I like spending time with my family, hiking, making photographs, rock climbing, badminton, eating Korean food, and playing with computers. My favorite websites are [Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/news) and [Gelman's blog](https://statmodeling.stat.columbia.edu/) and my favorite TV show is [Sunny](https://itsalwayssunny.fandom.com/wiki/It%27s_Always_Sunny_in_Philadelphia_Wiki).
